TSLA: Tesla to End One-Time FSD Purchase: Why the Shift Matters. Tesla is discontinuing its one-time Full Self-Driving (FSD) purchase option after February 14, 2026, shifting exclusively to a monthly subscription model. This change aims to provide more stable recurring revenue, align with CEO Elon Musk's long-term compensation goals tied to FSD subscriptions, and mitigate issues around hardware compatibility and customer expectations of a "forever promise" for autonomy. The move also positions Tesla within an increasingly competitive landscape for advanced driver assistance systems, with rivals like Rivian and NVIDIA offering their own solutions.

Cathie Wood Dumps $38 Million Worth Of Tesla Stock Amid Robotaxi Optimism, Buys Shares of This Chipmaker Despite China Crackdown. Cathie Wood's Ark Invest sold $37.8 million worth of Tesla stock, even as analyst Dan Ives predicted Tesla could control 80% of the robotaxi market. Concurrently, Ark Invest bought $48.6 million in Broadcom shares despite recent declines due to a Chinese security directive affecting VMware. Ark also divested $6.3 million in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. shares, which has struggled to meet production demands from clients like Nvidia and Broadcom.

S&P Global Mobility 2025 Loyalty Awards Reveal Divergent Paths to Customer Retention; General Motors and Tesla Secure Top Honors. S&P Global Mobility announced the winners of its 30th annual Automotive Loyalty Awards, recognizing General Motors for "Overall Loyalty to Manufacturer" for the 11th consecutive year and Tesla for "Overall Loyalty to Make." The awards, based on 13.6 million new retail vehicle registrations, highlight diverse strategies for customer retention, with product redesigns, dealer performance, and targeted market appeal proving successful. Tesla also secured awards for "Highest Conquest %" and "Ethnic Loyalty to Make," demonstrating its sustained market impact.

Ark de Cathie Wood vende ações da Tesla e compra Broadcom e Klarna. Cathie Wood's ARK ETFs made significant portfolio adjustments on January 14, 2026. The firm sold 86,139 shares of Tesla (TSLA) and 19,310 shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co Ltd (TSM), while making substantial purchases of Broadcom Inc (AVGO) and Klarna Group PLC (KLAR). These moves indicate a strategic rebalancing towards fintech and other technology sectors, reducing exposure to established tech giants.

cathie wood’s ark sells tesla stock, buys broadcom and klarna By Investing.com. Cathie Wood's ARK ETFs executed significant trades on January 14, 2026, selling 86,139 shares of Tesla Inc. worth $38.5 million from its ARKK ETF. Concurrently, ARK made substantial purchases, acquiring 143,089 shares of Broadcom Inc. for $50.7 million across its ARKK and ARKW ETFs, and 56,993 shares of Klarna Group PLC for $1.7 million through its ARKF ETF. These moves indicate a strategic rebalancing towards fintech and other technology sectors, reducing exposure to established tech giants.

cathie wood’s ark sells tesla stock, buys broadcom and klarna. Cathie Wood's ARK ETF has sold a significant portion of its Tesla (TSLA) stock, amounting to over $38.5 million, marking a reduction in a long-held staple of its portfolio. Concurrently, ARK made substantial purchases of Broadcom (AVGO) and Klarna (KLAR), investing over $50 million and $1.7 million respectively, indicating a strategic shift towards growth prospects in semiconductors and fintech. These trades highlight ARK's rebalancing strategy, divesting from established tech giants and increasing exposure to evolving market sectors.

Stellantis N.V. Is Quietly Eating Detroit’s Lunch – Should You Jump In Now or Nah?. This article analyzes Stellantis N.V. as a potentially undervalued automotive giant that is aggressively pivoting to electric vehicles while its stock remains quiet despite the strong cultural presence of its brands like Jeep, Dodge, and Ram. It discusses the company's EV strategy, brand power, and valuation compared to competitors like Tesla, Ford, and GM, concluding it could be a "cop" for long-term investors seeking value rather than a "meme rocket."

2 EV Stocks That Could Be Heading for $0, and 1 With Multibagger Potential Left. The U.S. EV market faced headwinds in 2025, with sales down 1% due to tariffs and the expiration of tax credits. While Rivian and Lucid struggle with high losses despite revenue growth, Tesla, despite a challenging year, shows strong fundamentals and promising Q3 2025 revenue growth, positioning it as the most viable long-term investment among American EV manufacturers.

Waymo rivals Tesla by expanding service area in Austin. Waymo has expanded its driverless ride-hailing service in Austin, now covering 140 square miles, to compete with Tesla's robotaxi service. While Tesla boasts a larger service area of over 245 square miles, Waymo has more active robotaxis on Austin's streets with approximately 200 cars. This expansion is part of Waymo's broader growth strategy, with plans to launch services in several other U.S. cities, including Dallas, Houston, and San Antonio, this year.

Robotaxis, Data Highways, Semiconductor Surge: Peraso (NASDAQ: PRSO) Tipping the Spear for NVDA, TSLA, UBER and more….. Peraso Inc. (NASDAQ: PRSO) is highlighted as a critical player in the accelerating robotaxi revolution by providing high-speed data transmission infrastructure. Their 60 GHz millimeter-wave chips address the massive data offload bottlenecks faced by autonomous vehicle fleets, enabling faster AI model retraining and improved efficiency. The article positions Peraso as an essential enabler of the robotaxi market's scalability, contributing to the overlooked semiconductor demand for advanced communications within these mobile data centers.

Robotaxis, Data Highways, Semiconductor Surge: Peraso (NASDAQ: PRSO) Tipping the Spear for NVDA, TSLA, UBER and more….. The article highlights Peraso Inc. (NASDAQ: PRSO) as a key player in the robotaxi ecosystem, focusing on its 60 GHz millimeter-wave (mmWave) semiconductor solutions that address critical data transmission bottlenecks. These solutions enable ultra-high-speed wireless data offload for autonomous vehicle fleets, drastically reducing the time needed to upload massive amounts of data generated by robotaxis. This efficiency is crucial for accelerating AI model retraining and map updates, positioning Peraso as an essential component for the scalable deployment of autonomous vehicles, complementing major players like NVIDIA, Tesla, and Uber.

Tesla puts full self-driving behind a monthly paywall. Tesla has announced that its Full Self-Driving (Supervised) software will transition to a monthly subscription model starting February 14, discontinuing the one-time purchase option. This move, driven in part by a recent shareholder-approved pay package for CEO Elon Musk tied to achieving 10 million FSD subscriptions, coincides with increased competition and scrutiny over the software's capabilities and marketing. The change reflects Tesla's ongoing strategy to monetize its advanced driver-assistance features amidst challenges in its EV market share and robotaxi development.

Elon Musk must deliver on Tesla's Robotaxi promise in 2026, Deutsche Bank says. Deutsche Bank analysts believe that 2026 is a crucial year for Tesla to deliver on its Robotaxi promise, especially as the company faces declining car deliveries and questions about its core automotive business. Despite Elon Musk's previous overly optimistic projections and subsequent caution regarding autonomous deployment, investors are expected to focus on Robotaxi expansion and humanoid development to offset weaknesses in auto sales. The article highlights Tesla's struggles in China and Europe and emphasizes that over 90% of its revenue still comes from cars, making the success of Robotaxis critical for future growth and investor confidence.

First Tesla Cybertruck Lands in Kenya. The first Tesla Cybertruck has arrived in Kenya as part of a Guinness World Record attempt by the global road expedition team CyberLocos. Adventurers Berni and Alex are driving the Cybertruck across several continents to showcase its endurance and promote clean energy. The vehicle, spotted in Mombasa and Nairobi, has generated significant excitement and speculation among Kenyans.

Flying panels to bad glue: Recalls for Tesla's 'indestructible' Cybertruck pile up. Tesla's Cybertruck, advertised as "indestructible," has faced numerous recalls since its late 2023 release, totaling nearly 116,000 vehicles recalled in 2025 alone. Issues have ranged from flying stainless steel panels due to faulty adhesive and dislodging accelerator pads to overly bright headlights and problems with rearview cameras and windshield wipers. Despite the mounting recalls and a 38% drop in sales by Q3 2025, the vehicle maintains a following among loyalists, though Tesla is now focusing on its upcoming Cybercab to boost sales.

Tech Voices: DeSantis vs. AI, Tesla's FSD, smart glasses market. Florida Governor Ron DeSantis has called for increased regulation of AI and data centers, citing them as a threat to the U.S. economy. This statement is part of Seeking Alpha's roundup of significant announcements and remarks affecting the technology sector. The article also touches upon Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology and the smart glasses market.

Tesla reportedly struggling to offload inventory in India amid cancellations By Investing.com. Tesla is reportedly having difficulty selling about one-third of the initial Model Y vehicles imported to India last year, with many early reservation holders canceling orders. This struggle comes as Tesla faces declining global demand and was surpassed by BYD Co. as the top-selling EV manufacturer in 2025. To boost sales in India, where import taxes are high and prices start at nearly $70,000, Tesla is offering discounts of up to 200,000 rupees on certain Model Y variants.

Tesla’s India Letdown Spurs Discounts on Unsold Model Y SUVs. Tesla is reportedly facing sluggish demand for its Model Y SUVs in India, with about a third of its initial imports remaining unsold four months after shipment. This has led the company to offer discounts of up to 200,000 rupees ($2,200) on some variants to clear the inventory. The issue arises as some early bookers have withdrawn their purchases, leaving approximately 100 vehicles without buyers.
TGT: ButcherBox Launches in Target. ButcherBox, a leading direct-to-consumer meat and seafood brand, has launched its fresh, 100% grass-fed, grass-finished beef products in over 1,400 Target stores nationwide. This marks ButcherBox's first major retail expansion beyond its subscription service, aiming to make its rigorously sourced proteins more accessible to a wider consumer base. The move aligns with Target's growing focus on wellness-focused offerings and addresses consumer demand for healthier meat options available for in-store grocery shopping.

Assessing Ulta Beauty (ULTA) Valuation As New Brand Launches And Events Support Growth Story. Ulta Beauty (ULTA) is gaining attention due to new brand launches, upcoming events, and store expansion. Despite recent strong stock performance and trading near all-time highs, Simply Wall St's analysis suggests Ulta is currently overvalued with a fair value of $603 compared to its closing price of US$664.85. The article highlights both growth opportunities, like international expansion, and potential risks, such as rising costs and the Target partnership's future.

Citigroup Lowers Kimberly-Clark (NASDAQ:KMB) Price Target to $90.00. Citigroup has lowered its price target for Kimberly-Clark (NASDAQ:KMB) to $90.00 from $95.00, maintaining a "sell" rating, which implies a potential 9.2% downside. The broader analyst consensus, according to MarketBeat, is a "Hold" with an average target of $120.00. Kimberly-Clark recently exceeded quarterly earnings estimates, reporting $1.82 EPS against an anticipated $1.45, with revenue slightly above expectations.

Minnesota’s biggest companies starting to feel heat from ICE surge. Minnesota's largest employers, including Target, General Mills, and Caribou Coffee, are facing significant pressure due to a surge of federal immigration officers in the state following a recent killing. Businesses are dealing with detained employees, grappling with public communications, and trying to protect their workforces amidst increased ICE presence. This situation is leading to concerns about economic growth, job stability, and diversity within Minnesota's industries.
SBUX: Nike vs. Starbucks: Which Turnaround Effort Is More Likely to Succeed?. This article compares the turnaround efforts of Nike and Starbucks, both iconic companies struggling with growth and margin pressure. It highlights their new CEOs' strategies and initial progress, noting that while both face challenges, Nike is considered more likely to succeed due to its strong brand resonance with consumers, especially compared to Starbucks' significant challenges in the Chinese market and greater pressure on its gross profit margins.

SBUX - Starbucks Corp Latest Stock News & Market Updates. This page provides the latest news and market updates for Starbucks Corporation (SBUX), covering earnings reports, dividend announcements, strategic partnerships, and international expansion efforts. It serves as a comprehensive resource for investors and traders to track the company's financial performance and business developments. Recent announcements include Q1 FY2026 earnings call details, a quarterly cash dividend declaration, and a significant joint venture in China.

VCI Global Secures First Enterprise Asset Supply Partner for Its RWA Exchange, Unlocking Global Merchant Network Including Starbucks and Pizza Hut. VCI Global Limited has announced its first enterprise asset supply partner for its Real World Asset (RWA) Exchange, collaborating with Mezzofy Holding Limited. This partnership integrates high-frequency consumer commerce assets, specifically merchant vouchers from global brands like Starbucks and Pizza Hut, into VCI Global's RWA ecosystem. The initiative aims to tokenize these assets, enabling compliant exchange and settlement, thereby bridging traditional commerce with the emerging RWA economy.

Hear Starbucks Q1 2026 results on its Jan. 28 webcast call live online. Starbucks Corporation (Nasdaq: SBUX) will release its first quarter fiscal year 2026 financial results on January 28, 2026, at 7:45 a.m. Eastern Time. A conference call will follow at 8:00 a.m. Eastern Time, which will be webcast live on the company's investor relations website, with a replay available until March 13, 2025.

Starbucks and Pizza Hut vouchers feed into $8B tokenized asset plan. VCI Global (NASDAQ: VCIG) has announced a partnership with Mezzofy, a digital voucher platform, to onboard its first enterprise asset supply partner for its Real World Asset (RWA) Exchange. Mezzofy, which processes over $8 billion in voucher value from brands like Starbucks and Pizza Hut, will supply redeemable merchant vouchers. Smart Bridge Technologies, a VCI Global subsidiary, will provide the tokenization, custody, and settlement infrastructure for these assets, aiming to bridge traditional commerce with the emerging RWA economy.
DIS: Rep. Julia Letlow Purchases Shares of The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS). Representative Julia Letlow (R-Louisiana) recently disclosed a purchase of between $1,001 and $15,000 in Walt Disney (NYSE: DIS) stock on November 3rd, made through her Merrill Lynch account. This transaction is part of several buys and sells of different stocks she made in December 2025 and earlier. The article details Disney's recent financial performance, including beating EPS estimates, and highlights analyst ratings, stock performance, and hedge fund activities related to DIS.

Walt Disney Company Names Asad Ayaz as First-Ever Chief Marketing Officer. The Walt Disney Company has appointed Asad Ayaz as its first-ever Chief Marketing and Brand Officer, elevating him to a company-wide role focusing on brand consistency and audience engagement. Ayaz, who previously led marketing for The Walt Disney Studios, will now oversee all Disney marketing teams and report directly to CEO Bob Iger and other senior leaders. This move aims to streamline Disney's marketing efforts across its various segments and build stronger connections with its global audience.

The Walt Disney Company Announces Date for Q1 2026 Earnings Call. The Walt Disney Company announced it will host a live audio webcast on Monday, February 2, 2026, to discuss its fiscal first quarter 2026 financial results. The webcast will begin at 8:30 a.m. ET and will cover financial and operational performance, including streaming and parks, and potentially include an announcement regarding Bob Iger's successor as CEO. Josh D’Maro is a highly favored candidate for the CEO position.

The Walt Disney Company Sets Fiscal First Quarter 2026 Call Date. The Walt Disney Company has announced its fiscal first quarter 2026 financial results webcast for Monday, February 2, 2026, at 8:30 a.m. ET. This Q1 period, covering October, November, and December, is crucial as it includes the holiday season and major film releases, making it a key indicator of the company's annual performance for investors and analysts. The article highlights potential discussion points such as theme park performance, Avatar box office results, CEO succession, and the impact of AI partnerships.

Walt Disney Company nomeia Asad Ayaz como primeiro diretor de marketing. The Walt Disney Company has appointed Asad Ayaz to the newly created role of Chief Marketing and Brand Officer, marking the first time the company has installed a CMO for its entire operations. Ayaz, who previously oversaw marketing for Walt Disney Studios, will now lead a consolidated internal marketing organization aiming for a more connected approach across all Disney segments. This strategic move, announced by CEO Bob Iger, seeks to strengthen brand consistency and consumer interaction as Disney prepares for a significant year of theatrical releases in 2026.

The Walt Disney Company Establishes New Enterprise Marketing Organization; Names Asad Ayaz Chief Marketing and Brand Officer. The Walt Disney Company has created a new enterprise marketing and brand organization to better align its marketing teams and strengthen its connection with consumers globally. Asad Ayaz, who has been with Disney for over two decades, has been appointed Chief Marketing and Brand Officer to lead this new organization. This move aims to ensure brand consistency and enhance how Disney reaches audiences and advances its business objectives across all segments.

The Walt Disney Company Names First-Ever Chief Marketing Officer. The Walt Disney Company has appointed Asad Ayaz, former head of marketing for The Walt Disney Studios, as its first-ever Chief Marketing Officer (CMO). This historic move marks the first time the company has established a CMO role across its entire operations. Ayaz brings eight years of experience leading marketing efforts for major Disney properties including Marvel, Lucasfilm, Pixar, and Disney's live-action and animation studios.

Asad Ayaz to Lead Disney’s New Company-Wide Marketing Operation. Asad Ayaz has been appointed as Disney's first-ever chief marketing and brand officer, leading a newly created conglomerate-wide marketing organization. This strategic move aims to align Disney's marketing teams across its diverse businesses, from film and television to theme parks and ESPN, and enhance its global audience connection. Ayaz will report to CEO Bob Iger and the segment chairs, leveraging his extensive experience to strengthen the Disney brand worldwide.

Loop Industries Reports Third Quarter Fiscal 2026 Results. Loop Industries, Inc. announced its financial results for the third quarter of fiscal year 2026, reporting increased revenues, decreased operating expenses, and a significant reduction in net loss. The company also provided updates on its Infinite Loop India project and European partnership, including an off-take agreement with Nike and progress in financing and site selection. Daniel Solomita, CEO, highlighted milestones in India, and the company expects continued revenue from engineering services and licensing payments to support ongoing operations.

Loop Industries, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report. Loop Industries, Inc. has released its Q3 2024 Form 10-Q report, detailing financial and operational progress. The company reported $0.086 million in total revenues, a net loss of $2.944 million, and a net loss per share of $(0.06). Key advancements include the launch of Twist™ recycled polyester resin, progress on an Infinite Loop™ manufacturing facility in India, and new agreements with Nike, Inc. and Taro Plast S.p.A.
